events.title: LakeApalooza
events.location: Green Lake American Legion Parking Lot
events.venue_name: Green Lake American Legion Parking Lot
events.description: Join us (rain or shine) for the annual LakeApalooza Green Lake Community Street Dance on Saturday, September 5! This fundraising event for Thrasher Opera House, The Green Lake American Legion, and the Green Lake Area Chamber of Commerce is a fun family event you want to attend! All the fun begins at 5:00 pm downtown Green Lake by the American Legion. Live music by Davidson County Band starts at 7:00 pm. Come hungry, as there will be many food choices. A full bar will be at the American Legion Post 306. Let’s end our Green Lake summer with a bang and enjoy it together in downtown Green Lake. Hope to see you there! Attendees may bring their own chairs This fantastic event is brought to you by Mike & Jill Havey Please direct questions regarding this event to the Green Lake Chamber of Commerce: 920.294.3231 or info@visitgreenlake.com

events.title: Angler Young Angler Fishing Tounament
events.location: Deacon Mills Park, Green Lake WI
events.venue_name: Deacon Mills Park, Green Lake WI
events.description: For many children in the Green Lake area, the annual Angler & Young Angler Fishing Tournament on Big Green Lake marks the opening of fishing season. It serves as the foundation to happy memories of spending time on the water with family and friends that will last a lifetime. The goal of this event is “to promote the Green Lake community and the great outdoors by introducing youth to the sport of fishing on Big Green Lake.” Each team consists of one adult angler and up to two young anglers, ages 4 to 17. From the 8:30am blast-off to the 3:30pm weigh-in, young anglers spend time fishing with fathers, mothers or mentors exploring the waters in search of the right combination of 5 smallmouth bass needed to take first place.

events.title: Taste & Tunes | Green Lake Wine Walk
events.location: Downtown Green Lake
events.venue_name: Downtown Green Lake
events.description: Dust off those leg warmers, tease up that hair, and get ready to party like it's the '80s! 🪩🍷🎶 The Green Lake Area Chamber of Commerce is excited to host the Fourth Annual Taste & Tunes Wine Walk on Friday, August 7, 2026, from 4:30 PM – 8:00 PM in Downtown Green Lake. Neon everywhere, live tunes, great wine, and big hair are taking over Downtown Green Lake for one unforgettable evening. Participating businesses will be serving a variety of unique wine samples while embracing this year's all-out '80s theme. Stop locations and entertainment details will be announced closer to the event. 🎟️ Tickets go on sale Thursday, June 11 at 12:00 PM. Your ticket includes: 🍷 A variety of unique wine samples at participating stops 🥤 A custom Green Lake Wine Walk tumbler, sponsored by Solara Aevum Wellness & Aesthetics 👜 The first 70 registrations will receive a FREE 2025 Wine Walk tote bag Throw on your best '80s attire, call up your friends, and get ready to stroll from stop to stop as our decked-out businesses bring the decade to life. From unique wine samples and live tunes to shopping and plenty of throwback fun, it's sure to be an unforgettable summer evening in Downtown Green Lake. Must be 21 years of age or older to attend. Tickets are non-refundable.

events.title: GLA 13dot1 Races
events.location: Town Square Terrace
events.venue_name: Town Square Terrace
events.description: Green Lake Area 13dot1 Races – Half Marathon, 10K & 5K Kick off Labor Day Weekend in scenic Green Lake with your choice of a Half Marathon, 10K, or 5K —all starting and finishing at the Town Square Community Center Terrace. Our race courses wind through historic downtown, past local shops and parks, and along the breathtaking shoreline of Big Green Lake. Runners will enjoy a mix of charming neighborhoods, lakefront vistas, and wooded pathways, with the Half Marathon featuring an extended tour through the beautiful Green Lake Conference Center grounds. All routes offer family-friendly fun, community spirit, and memorable views, ending back at Town Square for post-race celebrations.

events.location: Deacon Mills Park Green Lake, WI 54941
events.venue_name: Deacon Mills Park Green Lake, WI 54941
events.description: The Wisconsin State Chili Cook-Off is the only sanctioned chili cook-off in Wisconsin . State champions earn the right to advance to the International Chili Society World Championship, making this a must-attend event for chili lovers. Come taste award-winning chili, enjoy the competition, and be part of one of Wisconsin’s most flavorful traditions.

events.title: Kronos Quartet
events.location:
Demmer Recital Hall – Rodman Center for the Arts, Ripon College
400 Union St.
Ripon, WI 54971
events.venue_name:
Demmer Recital Hall – Rodman Center for the Arts, Ripon College
400 Union St.
Ripon, WI 54971
events.description: A centerpiece of the season, the legendary Kronos Quartet makes its first appearance at the Green Lake Festival of Music, bringing five decades of fearless artistry to the stage. The San Francisco–based ensemble has redefined the string quartet through bold programming, global collaborations, and more than seventy boundary-breaking recordings. Audiences are invited to a pre-concert talk with the performers at 7:00 p.m., followed by a free evening concert marked by intensity, curiosity, and musical discovery. This appearance carries special resonance for GLFM. Departing Artistic & Executive Director Sam Handley reflects, “A centerpiece event is the debut of the Kronos Quartet—their first appearance at GLFM. Three members of the groundbreaking ensemble previously served on the faculty of GLFM’s Chamber Music Institute (CMI).” Gabriela Díaz, Ayane Kozasa, and Paul Wiancko have each played meaningful roles within the Institute, with Díaz returning in consecutive post-pandemic seasons to perform and collaborate during a pivotal period for the festival. Their continued relationship underscores the festival’s theme of connection — artists who teach, perform, and grow alongside the GLFM community. Featuring David Harrington with Díaz, Kozasa, and Wiancko, Kronos brings its unmistakable voice to this free performance that unites international influence with deep institutional ties, offering audiences a rare convergence of innovation, history, and shared artistic roots. https://greenlakefestival.org/event/kronos_quartet/

events.title: Chamber Music Institute Second Faculty Concert
events.location:
Demmer Recital Hall – Rodman Center for the Arts, Ripon College
400 Union St.
Ripon, WI 54971
events.venue_name:
Demmer Recital Hall – Rodman Center for the Arts, Ripon College
400 Union St.
Ripon, WI 54971
events.description: A marvelous mix of professional chamber musicians explores duos, trios, and quintet repertoire in this GLFM Chamber Music Institute faculty concert, celebrating both tradition and today’s creative voices as part of the America 250 observance. The program highlights the vitality of contemporary American composition with Kenji Bunch’s Three American Folk Hymns and Melia Watras’ dynamic Kreutzer , spotlighting the evolving sound of American classical music. Rebecca Clarke’s expressive Two Pieces for Viola and Cello offers a graceful nod to America’s English roots, while Mozart’s dramatic String Quintet in G minor brings Classical brilliance to close the evening. Performed by GLFM faculty artists — Salley Koo and Lee Taylor, violins; Jesse Holstein, viola; Elizabeth Oakes, viola; and Gregory Sauer, cello — this concert reflects the Institute’s commitment to learning through instruction, collaboration, and example while honoring the diverse influences that shape American musical identity. Approximate program length: 65 minutes. https://greenlakefestival.org/event/cmi-second-faculty-concert/

events.description: Join us for Yoga in the Vineyard! Our popular weekly yoga class, with Lotus Root Yoga, held outdoors among the grapevines is coming back this summer! This gentle Vinyasa flow class is suitable for all levels. Be sure to bring your yoga mat, sunglasses, and plenty of water. The cost is $10 per class (option to register and pay online) Link to register online : https://clients.mindbodyonline.com/classic/ws?studioid=640759&stype=-7&sTG=23&sView=day&sLoc=0&date=06/18/26 Each class is led by a rotating team of instructors. Yoga goes from 5:30pm - 6:30pm. In the case of inclement weather, class will be cancelled. The winery is open until 9PM, with a selection of beverages, snacks, pizza (served until 8PM), and other food options. (outside food and drinks are not permitted) We can't wait to see you at this relaxing outdoor series every Thursday until the end of August!

events.title: Summer Radiance Kundalini Yoga
events.location:
Green Lake Town Square Studio A
492 Hill St
Green Lake, Wi 54941
events.venue_name:
Green Lake Town Square Studio A
492 Hill St
Green Lake, Wi 54941
events.description: Summer Radiance A 6-Week Elemental Journey and Kick off Immersion Join us for a soulful summer journey designed to awaken your inner radiance through Kundalini Yoga, breathwork, meditation, mantra, and sacred movement. Beginning with a special 90-minute Kickoff Immersion on Thursday, June 18 from 6-7:30pm, this experience invites you into deeper connection with yourself, the natural elements, and the wisdom of the body. Together we will gather weekly on Thursday evenings for six weeks of movement, breath, reflection, and energetic renewal. Then following every Thursday from 6-7pm. EACH WEEK EXPLORES A DIFFERENT ELEMENTAL THEME: • Earth — grounding, presence, and connection • Water — emotional flow, creativity, and softness • Fire — transformation, courage, and vitality • Air — heart opening, expansion, and breath • Sound — vibration, voice, and expression • Light — intuition, integration, and inner radiance This series is open to all experience levels and offers a nurturing space for healing, self-discovery, embodiment, and community connection. LOCATION: Town Square Community Center Green Lake, WI INVESTMENT: Full Series Pass: $90 (includes kickoff + all 6 weeks) Single Session Drop-In: $18 Kickoff Immersion Only: $25 Participants may register by scanning the QR code on promotional materials and completing registration online.  Whether you are seeking renewal, deeper connection, healing, or simply a space to breathe and reconnect with yourself, you are warmly welcomed exactly as you are on this journey.

events.venue_name:

events.description: Returning to the Green Lake Festival of Music after their 2025 Gala appearance, the Madison-based Avanti Piano Trio continues a shared commitment to the piano-trio tradition at the heart of GLFM’s chamber music legacy. Pianist Alissa Freeman, violinist Hillary Hempel, and cellist Hannah Wolkstein bring “impressive energy and musicality” to a program that balances beloved repertoire with under-explored works, including music by female composers. This return reflects the festival’s 2026 theme of artistic connection. As departing Artistic & Executive Director Sam Handley notes, “Returning after their 2025 Gala appearance, the Madison-based trio shares GLFM’s long-standing focus on piano-trio repertoire.” Since forming in Madison in 2018, the Avanti Piano Trio has performed throughout Wisconsin. Their appearance affirms both continuity and discovery — a familiar ensemble deepening its relationship with GLFM while inviting audiences into the rich and evolving world of piano trio music. https://greenlakefestival.org/event/avanti-piano-trio-june-24-2026-first-congregational-ripon/

events.title: Art MidWisconsin: Hands on Art: Exploring the Human Form
events.location:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.venue_name:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.description: Dates: July 7 & 9 Time: 9 am - Noon Instructor: Travis Nygard Location: MakerSpace Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $120 Note: This includes a $25 material fee Artists have been fascinated by other people since the Stone Age, and in this class, we will use depictions of the human body as a case study. It is for people of all experience levels and combines interactive lectures, hands-on exercises, and art-making. You can expect to see examples of great paintings, drawings, and sculptures, learn how artists have represented the human face in many ways. You will learn how to draw a face with accurate proportions. You will also learn how artists thought about the body in layers from the inside out—from the skeleton, to muscles, to skin, to clothing. We will even consider how artists have used color and layers to depict people in both realistic and wildly unrealistic ways. The course is geared toward adults, but mature high-school-age students are also welcome to enroll. The course includes some artistic representations of unclothed bodies. No prior knowledge or artistic skill is required to enjoy this class. Art supplies will be provided, including a set of watercolor paints and oil pastels for you to keep.

events.location:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St

events.venue_name:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace

events.description: Acrylics Made Fun with an Exploration into Abstract Art Dates: July 13, 14 & 15 Time: 9 am - 1 pm Instructor: Anne Gary Location: MakerSpace Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $150 Note: A supply list will be provided upon registration Get ready to roll up your sleeves, splash some color around, and have a seriously fun time in this 4-hour acrylic painting class! Whether you’re a total beginner or someone who just hasn’t picked up a brush in a while, this session is all about letting your creativity loose in a relaxed, no-pressure atmosphere. We’ll kick things off with a quick intro to materials (don’t worry—nothing too technical), then dive right into playful demos where you’ll learn cool tricks like blending colors, creating texture, and bringing your painting to life layer by layer. Think of it as part lesson, part creative playground. As you paint, you’ll get plenty of guidance, tips, and encouragement—plus the freedom to add your own personal twist. There’s time to experiment, make happy little accidents, and discover your own style along the way. By the end of the class, you’ll walk away with a finished painting you can proudly show off—and maybe even surprise yourself with what you created.

events.description: Intro to Digital Photography Date: July 15 Time: 9 am - Noon Instructor: Eileen Reilly Location: Studio B Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $75 Note: DSLR cameras and Smartphones are welcome Whether you are picking up a camera for the first time or looking to sharpen your foundational skills, this course offers a welcoming and practical starting point for your photography journey. This 3 hour course us designed for beginners who want to move beyond automatic mode and start taking intentional and compelling photos. Students will learn to navigate their camera’s settings-specifically the exposure triangle: aperture, shutter speed and ISO. The principles of composition will be covered by exploring the rule of thirds, leading lines and framing to build more visually engaging images. There will be a brief overview of lighting, especially natural light. The course will finish with an outdoor shoot, where students will apply everything they have learned into a real-world environment.

A third-generation magician known for his critically acclaimed show The Magic Parlour in Chicago, Watkins delivers a dazzling mix of sleight-of-hand, mind-reading, storytelling, and humor that keeps audiences on the edge of their seats. This intimate live performance puts the magic right in front of you—sometimes even in your own hands—as impossible moments unfold just inches away. Watkins’ performances blend classic magic with modern wit, creating an interactive experience that will leave you laughing, questioning reality, and rediscovering a sense of childlike wonder. Whether you’re a longtime fan of magic or simply looking for a unique night out, this is a show you won’t want to miss.
